intitule: Exercise 1: Reading Comprehension - Biography of Nelson Mandela

enonce.texte:
Read the following biography of Nelson Mandela carefully and answer the questions.

NELSON MANDELA: A GREAT LEADER

Nelson Mandela was born on 18th July 1918 in South Africa. He was a lawyer and a freedom fighter who fought against apartheid, a system of racial segregation. Mandela believed that all people, regardless of their skin colour, should have equal rights.

In 1962, Mandela was arrested and sent to prison for 27 years. During his time in prison, he became a symbol of resistance against injustice. He never gave up his fight for freedom and equality.

In 1990, Mandela was released from prison. Four years later, in 1994, he became the first black president of South Africa. He worked to unite the country and promote peace. Mandela received the Nobel Peace Prize in 1993 for his efforts.

Nelson Mandela died on 5th December 2013. He is remembered as one of the greatest leaders in history. His famous words, "Learn about your history," inspire people all over the world.

questions.texte: When and where was Nelson Mandela born?
questions.reponse: Nelson Mandela was born on 18th July 1918 in South Africa.

questions.texte: What did Mandela fight against?
questions.reponse: Mandela fought against apartheid, a system of racial segregation in South Africa.

questions.texte: How long did Mandela spend in prison?
questions.reponse: Mandela spent 27 years in prison.

questions.texte: List two achievements of Nelson Mandela mentioned in the text.
questions.reponse: Two achievements of Nelson Mandela mentioned are: 1) He became the first black president of South Africa in 1994, and 2) He received the Nobel Peace Prize in 1993 for his efforts.

questions.texte: Analyze why Nelson Mandela is considered one of the greatest leaders in history.
questions.reponse: Nelson Mandela is considered one of the greatest leaders in history because he fought against apartheid and racial discrimination for 27 years in prison. He never gave up his fight for freedom and equality. After his release, he became the first black president of South Africa and worked to unite the country. He promoted peace and reconciliation, which earned him the Nobel Peace Prize. His leadership and sacrifice inspired people all over the world.

enonce.texte:
Complete the sentences using the imperative form of the verbs in brackets.

Remember: The imperative is used to give orders, instructions, or advice. It uses the base form of the verb.

questions.texte: ______ (learn) about your history to understand your culture better.
questions.reponse:
Learn

Explication: L'impératif utilise la forme de base du verbe. 'Learn' est utilisé pour donner un conseil ou une instruction.

questions.texte: ______ (not/give up) on your dreams, even when things are difficult.
questions.reponse:
Don't give up

Explication: L'impératif négatif se forme avec 'don't' + la forme de base du verbe. C'est un conseil pour persévérer.

questions.texte: ______ (respect) the leaders who fought for your freedom.
questions.reponse:
Respect

Explication: L'impératif 'Respect' donne une instruction ou un conseil sur le comportement à adopter.

questions.texte: ______ (read) books about famous leaders to learn from their experiences.
questions.reponse:
Read

Explication: L'impératif 'Read' donne un conseil pour s'informer.

questions.texte: ______ (not/forget) the sacrifices made by our leaders.
questions.reponse:
Don't forget

Explication: L'impératif négatif 'Don't forget' est utilisé pour rappeler quelque chose d'important.

enonce.texte:
Read the descriptions of three famous leaders and answer the questions.

Leader 1: Nelson Mandela - He was brave, patient, and forgiving. He fought for freedom and equality in South Africa.

Leader 2: Martin Luther King Jr. - He was courageous, inspiring, and peaceful. He fought for civil rights for African Americans in the USA.

Leader 3: Queen Nzinga - She was powerful, intelligent, and determined. She fought against Portuguese colonizers in Angola.

questions.texte: List three adjectives used to describe Nelson Mandela.
questions.reponse: Three adjectives used to describe Nelson Mandela are: brave, patient, and forgiving.

questions.texte: Compare Nelson Mandela and Martin Luther King Jr. What qualities do they share?
questions.reponse: Both Nelson Mandela and Martin Luther King Jr. were courageous and fought for equality and justice. Mandela was forgiving, while King was peaceful. Both leaders inspired people with their words and actions, and they both made great sacrifices for their people.

questions.texte: Which of these leaders do you admire most? Justify your answer.
questions.reponse:
Example answer:

I admire Nelson Mandela the most because he spent 27 years in prison fighting for freedom and equality. After his release, he did not seek revenge but instead worked to unite his country. His ability to forgive his enemies and promote peace is truly inspiring. He is a symbol of courage and patience.

intitule: Exercise 4: Reading - Speech of a Famous Leader

enonce.texte:
Read this excerpt from a famous speech and answer the questions.

EXCERPT FROM A SPEECH

"I have a dream that my four little children will one day live in a nation where they will not be judged by the colour of their skin but by the content of their character."

"Let us not seek to satisfy our thirst for freedom by drinking from the cup of bitterness and hatred."

questions.texte: Who do you think gave this speech?
questions.reponse: This speech was given by Martin Luther King Jr. It is from his famous 'I Have a Dream' speech.

questions.texte: Analyze the message of this speech. What did the leader want?
questions.reponse: In this speech, Martin Luther King Jr. wanted to express his dream of a world where people are not judged by the colour of their skin but by their character. He wanted equality and justice for all people. He also encouraged people to fight for freedom without using bitterness or hatred, but through peaceful means. The message is about hope, equality, and non-violent resistance.

questions.texte: Find one example of an imperative form in the text.
questions.reponse: The imperative form in the text is: "Let us not seek..." (or "Let us not..."). This is an exhortation or advice to the listeners.

intitule: Exercise 5: Writing - Biography of a Leader

enonce.texte:
Write a short biography (100-120 words) of a famous African or world leader you admire.

Your biography should include:

  • The leader's name, date and place of birth
  • What the leader is known for
  • At least 3 adjectives to describe the leader
  • One achievement of the leader
  • At least 2 imperative sentences (advice from the leader)

questions.texte: Write a biography of a famous leader.
questions.reponse:
Example Answer:

NELSON MANDELA - A HERO FOR FREEDOM

Nelson Mandela was born on 18th July 1918 in South Africa. He was a brave, patient, and forgiving leader who fought against apartheid. He spent 27 years in prison for fighting for equality and justice.

In 1994, Mandela became the first black president of South Africa. He worked to unite the country and promote peace. He received the Nobel Peace Prize in 1993.

Mandela inspired people all over the world with his words. Learn about your history to understand your identity. Never give up on your dreams, no matter how difficult they seem.

questions.texte:
Match:
1. Brave
2. Determined
3. Courageous
4. Wise
5. Inspiring
6. Peaceful

Definitions:
A. Having or showing experience, knowledge, and good judgment
B. Showing a willingness to take risks
C. Having made a firm decision
D. Filling someone with the urge or ability to do something
E. Not involving war or violence; calm
F. Not showing fear in the face of danger
questions.reponse:
1-F, 2-C, 3-B, 4-A, 5-D, 6-E

Explication: Ces adjectifs sont couramment utilisés pour décrire des leaders. 'Brave' et 'courageous' sont des synonymes. 'Determined' montre une forte volonté. 'Wise' indique de la sagesse. 'Inspiring' donne de l'inspiration. 'Peaceful' est lié à la non-violence.

enonce.texte:
Write a short paragraph (80-100 words) comparing two leaders you know about.

Your paragraph should include:

  • The names of the two leaders
  • What each leader is known for
  • Similarities and differences
  • At least 3 comparative adjectives (e.g., braver, more patient, less violent, etc.)

questions.texte: Write a paragraph comparing two leaders.
questions.reponse:
Example Answer:

Nelson Mandela and Martin Luther King Jr.

Nelson Mandela and Martin Luther King Jr. were both great leaders who fought for equality. Mandela was from South Africa and fought against apartheid, while King was from the USA and fought for civil rights. Mandela was more patient because he spent 27 years in prison. King was more peaceful because he used non-violent protests. However, both were more inspiring than many other leaders because their words and actions changed the world. They both believed in justice and equality for all people.

intitule: Exercise 8: Eulogy Writing - Tribute to a Leader

enonce.texte:
Write a short eulogy (120-150 words) for a famous leader who has passed away.

A eulogy should include:

  • The leader's name and a brief introduction
  • What the leader achieved in their life
  • Why the leader is remembered
  • A message or lesson we can learn from the leader
  • A final tribute

questions.texte: Write a eulogy for a famous leader.
questions.reponse:
Example Answer:

IN MEMORY OF NELSON MANDELA

Today, we gather to remember and honour Nelson Mandela, a man of courage, patience, and forgiveness. He was born in South Africa and dedicated his life to fighting against apartheid and racial injustice.

Mandela spent 27 years in prison, yet he never gave up his dream of freedom. He taught us that forgiveness is more powerful than revenge. He became the first black president of South Africa and worked to unite a divided nation.

Nelson Mandela inspired millions of people around the world. His words, "Learn about your history," remind us to understand our past to build a better future.

We will always remember him as a symbol of hope, peace, and resilience. Rest in peace, Madiba. Your legacy will live forever.

enonce.texte: Read the sentences below and identify the imperative forms. Write them down.

questions.texte:
Rewrite the following sentences as imperatives.
Example: 'You should learn about your history.' → 'Learn about your history.'

1. 'You should respect your leaders.' → ______________
2. 'You must not give up on your dreams.' → ______________
3. 'You should read about famous leaders.' → ______________
4. 'You must fight for your rights.' → ______________
5. 'You should not forget the sacrifices of our leaders.' → ______________
questions.reponse:
1. Respect your leaders.
2. Don't give up on your dreams.
3. Read about famous leaders.
4. Fight for your rights.
5. Don't forget the sacrifices of our leaders.

intitule: Exercise 10: Creative Writing - Letter to a Leader

enonce.texte:
Write a short letter (120-150 words) to a famous leader you admire. In your letter, explain why you admire them and ask them a question.

Your letter should include:

  • A proper greeting and closing
  • Why you admire the leader
  • What you have learned from them
  • A question for the leader
  • At least 2 imperative sentences

questions.texte: Write a letter to a famous leader.
questions.reponse:
Example Answer:

Dear Nelson Mandela,

I am writing to you to express my admiration for your courage and determination. You spent 27 years in prison fighting for freedom and equality, but you never gave up. I have learned from you that patience and forgiveness are powerful tools for change.

You taught us to stand up for our rights and to never forget our history. Your famous words, "Learn about your history," have inspired me to study the leaders of my country.

My question for you is: What advice would you give to young people who want to make a difference in their communities?

Thank you for being a symbol of hope and peace. Rest in peace, Madiba.

Yours sincerely,
Jean, Cameroon
